content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 9, 1989

Bonnie Knight
1100 N Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-209

Dear Ms. Knight:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding
permissible uses of campaign funds in conformance with the
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹

QUESTION

May a political committee or a broad based political committee contribute campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989,
to an unsuccessful 1988 Assembly campaign for the purpose of paying off campaign debts?

CONCLUSION

A political committee or a broad based political committee
may not contribute campaign funds received prior to January 1,
1989, to an unsuccessful 1988 Assembly campaign for the purpose of
paying off campaign debts.

ANALYSIS

Proposition 73, approved by the voters on June 7, 1988, and
operative January 1, 1989, regulates the use of campaign funds and
political contributions. Section 85306 permits the use of
campaign funds received prior to January 1, 1989, for any lawful
purpose other than to support or oppose a candidacy for elective
office.

___________________________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Bonnie Knight
May 9, 1989
Page 2


     The Commission has promulgated Regulation 18536.2 to imple-
ment the provisions of Section 85306. This regulation provides
that an expenditure to "support or oppose a candidacy for elective
office" includes:

          (1) Any monetary or nonmonetary contribution
     from a candidate or committee to another candidate
     for state or local office, a committee controlled
     by one or more candidates for state or local of-
     fice, or a committee formed primarily to support or
     oppose one or more candidates for state or local
     office.

                              (Regulation 18536.2(b)(1), copy
                              enclosed.)

     Section 82015 and Regulation 18215(a) (copy enclosed) define
"contribution" as "any monetary ... payment made for political
purposes for which full and adequate consideration is not made to
the donor."

     Pursuant to the above statutes and regulations, payments by a
political committee or broad based political committee to an
unsuccessful 1988 candidate are contributions if they are intended
to retire a campaign debt and no consideration is given therefor.
They would violate Section 85306 because they are contributions to
support or oppose a candidacy for elective office. As discussed
above, those contributions are prohibited.

     The Commission considered the adoption of an emergency amend-
ment to regulation 18536.2 at its regular meeting of May 2, 1989.
The proposed amendment deletes from Regulation 18536.2 sections
(b)(3) and (4), and section (c). These deletions, however, are
not applicable to your facts and adoption of the emergency regula-
tion would not alter our advice.

     Do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901 if I may be of
further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosures

FPPC
APR 10 11:27 AM '89


April 10, 1989


Ms. Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Ms. Breeze:

I am requesting confirmation of the use of restricted funds in a
specific instance.  A political committee or a broad base
political committee can contribute restricted funds to an
unsuccessful 1988 Assembly campaign to pay off the debts.
Per Reg. 1532, 2 EA

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Bonnie Knight
1100 N Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

E41-204

FPPC
APR 10 11:27 AM '89

April 10, 1989


Ms. Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Ms. Breeze:

I am requesting confirmation of the use of restricted funds in a
specific instance.  A political committee or a broad base
political committee can contribute restricted funds to an
unsuccessful 1988 Assembly campaign to pay off the debts.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Bonnie Knight
1100 N Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 12, 1989




Bonnie Knight
1100 N Steet
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-209

Dear Ms. Knight:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel
